milestone: Enrollment UX Hardening + PDF Layout Polish
description:
Version 1.8.0 consolidates roughly four weeks of incremental work across the enrollment flow, the generated PDF application, and the Netlify email backend. Baseline version audit against actual installed packages: bumped nodemailer from ^8.0.3 to ^8.0.5 and vite from ^8.0.1 to ^8.0.5 to match npm-resolved versions on the build machine. 

ENROLLMENT FORM (Enrollment.tsx) — Rewrote the submit handler into a two-phase flow: PDF generation and download always complete first (Step 1), then email delivery is attempted independently (Step 2); if email fails, the student still sees a success screen with a new amber 'Action Required' banner and direct mailto:/tel: buttons instead of a blocking error. Added dedicated emailWarning state decoupled from submitError. Replaced the opaque 'interaction-only' Turnstile widget with a callback-driven implementation: new turnstileToken, turnstileRendered, turnstileChallengeShown, and turnstileError states track the widget lifecycle; success, error, and expired callbacks update state; a MutationObserver detects when Cloudflare actually presents a challenge iframe so the submit gate only enforces the checkbox for users Cloudflare challenges (trusted users with silent tokens still submit instantly). Wired a new dynamic validation hint 'Complete the human verification' that appears in the pre-submit checklist only when Turnstile rendered AND a challenge was shown AND no token yet exists. Added email blur validation matching the phone/address pattern: new emailError state, EMAIL_PATTERN regex, handleEmailChange/handleEmailBlur handlers, red border + inline error message that clears on resumed typing. Rewrote phone formatting to be progressive: 1-3 digits render raw, 4-6 digits wrap in area-code parens, 7-10 digits add the hyphen; overflow past 10 digits now truncates (preserving the valid portion) rather than clearing the field, and the click-to-clear-on-error behavior was removed so users can correct in place. PDF filename format standardized to 'Ameristar-<mm-dd-yyyy>-<first-name>.pdf'. 

PDF GENERATION (utils/enrollmentPDF.ts) — Added a subtle muted-gold school address line (120 S. Del Mar Ave, Unit 1143, San Gabriel, CA 91778) rendered at 6.5pt inside the navy header bar on every page. Added fmtDate helper converting ISO yyyy-mm-dd to mm-dd-yyyy for both the Date of Application title block and the signature row. Replaced the two-line '[SPONSOR NUMBER] / [CE SPONSOR NUMBER]' placeholder with a single centered 'DRE Sponsor #: S0684' line. Reworked Section 04 policy clause rendering: replaced the PolicyClause.lines[] array of pre-wrapped strings with PolicyClause.text single-string fields, and replaced the manual line-by-line render with doc.splitTextToSize(clause.text, CW - PAD_L) so text auto-wraps at the true content-width margin instead of wrapping prematurely at conservative hardcoded break points. Resolved descender clipping (lowercase g/j/p/q/y being cut off) by restructuring the drawStripe geometry: removed the -1mm top overlap that was painting over descenders of the previous line, set LINE_H to 4.0mm for single-line spacing on 7.8pt text, and anchored the text baseline at an absolute y+2.5 position that is safe under both jsPDF y-coordinate conventions. Added a 0.4mm SEAM_OVERLAP extension to the stripe rect and gold bar so adjacent same-color stripes overlap imperceptibly, eliminating the hairline white seam PDF viewers render between contiguous rectangles — specifically fixing the visible gap between each policy subtitle and its description. Removed the 2mm gap between the heading stripe and the first body-line stripe so the tan background and gold left accent bar are unbroken per clause. Tightened Section 03 payment box geometry: boxH shrunk from 62mm/76mm (Zelle/CC) to 47mm/56mm, and reduced multiple internal gaps (pre-divider 2mm->1mm, post-divider 6mm->4mm, label-to-value 6mm->5mm, value-to-bottom 6mm->4mm, bottom pads 3mm->2mm) to recover roughly 22mm of vertical space in the Zelle case and 12mm in the CC case; combined with the auto-wrapped policy lines this reliably keeps the School Authorized Representative signature block on page 3 instead of overflowing to a 4th page for typical submissions. 

NETLIFY FUNCTION (netlify/functions/send-application.js) — Hardened the existing Turnstile block to be soft-optional: when the Turnstile token is absent or null in the request body (common when Cloudflare silent-passes a user or when a token expires mid-form), the backend logs a warning via console.warn but proceeds with the send instead of returning 403 'Bot verification token missing.' The honeypot and 5-requests-per-IP-per-minute rate limiter already protect the endpoint, so a missing token no longer hard-blocks legitimate submissions. A present-but-invalid token is still rejected. The CORS, origin validation, input sanitizer, email format validator, and existing Gmail/nodemailer delivery path are all unchanged. 

VERSION ALIGNMENT — Synchronized project version across package.json and milestone.json (both now 1.8.0). Validated all dependency versions in milestone.json against actual installed versions from npm list on the build machine (Node 24.15.0, npm 11.12.1). 

DEPENDENCY REFRESH (2026-04-19) — Updated multiple packages and runtime settings to their latest installed versions, verified by npm list --depth=0 on the build machine. React bumped from 19.2.4 to 19.2.5 (exact-pin, no caret). react-dom bumped from 19.2.4 to 19.2.5 (exact-pin, no caret). Vite bumped from ^8.0.5 to ^8.0.9 (caret range). @types/node tightened from ^24.0.0 to ^24.12.2 (matching the currently resolved 24.12.2 as the new floor). TypeScript underwent a major-version upgrade from ~5.9.3 to ~6.0.3, TypeScript 6.0 being the final release on the JavaScript-based compiler codebase before the Go-based TypeScript 7.0 ships. Node runtime promoted from 24.14.0 to 24.15.0 on both fronts: netlify.toml NODE_VERSION bumped to 24.15.0 so the Netlify build server uses the same Node version as the local development machine, and package.json engines tightened from >=24.14.0 to >=24.15.0 to make 24.15.0 a hard floor going forward. Compatibility verified: npx tsc --noEmit ran clean with zero errors across the entire project after the TypeScript 6 upgrade, confirming none of the 6.0 default-value changes or deprecations affect the current codebase. 

LUCIDE MAJOR UPGRADE (2026-04-19, evening) — Lucide-React upgraded from ^0.577.0 to ~1.8.0, crossing a major version boundary (0.x to 1.x). All icon imports used by the project (Send, CheckCircle, Loader2, AlertCircle, Info, BookOpen, Clock, and others) continued to resolve correctly under the new API. Compatibility verified twice: npx tsc --noEmit ran clean with zero errors, and npm run build completed successfully producing a functional production bundle (1941 modules transformed, 622ms build time). Tilde pinning (~1.8.0) was chosen over caret to match the conservative pinning pattern used for the TypeScript 6 upgrade — accepts 1.8.x patches but requires explicit upgrade for 1.9+. 

Project version bumped from 1.8.0 to 1.9.0 to reflect the dependency refresh and Lucide major upgrade. No functional runtime or code changes in this bump — package and tooling updates only.
